description: TUI-first, AI-assisted product status dashboard for a startup head of product. Go backend (dashboard-server) + Bubble Tea TUI client (dashboard-tui). Manages ~4 teams, 25-50 people with real-time view of sprint status, workload, velocity, and business metrics.

userStories.title: Project Scaffolding
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need a runnable Go repo with config loading, .env loading, and placeholder server and TUI binaries so that subsequent phases have a clean foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: go mod init github.com/your-org/dashboard executed; go.mod present

Directory tree created: cmd/server/main.go, cmd/tui/main.go, internal/api/, internal/auth/, internal/config/, internal/connector/github/, internal/connector/notion/, internal/connector/grafana/, internal/connector/posthog/, internal/connector/signoz/, internal/ai/, internal/pipeline/, internal/store/, internal/sync/, internal/tui/
internal/config/config.go defines Config, ServerConfig, StorageConfig, AuthConfig, AIConfig structs; Load(path string) (*Config, error) uses gopkg.in/yaml.v3
config.example.yaml present with all fields: server.port, storage.path, auth.jwt_secret, auth.admin_username, auth.admin_password_hash, ai.provider, ai.model, ai.api_key, ai.binary_path

internal/config/env.go: reads .env via os.ReadFile + manual key=value parse (no third-party library); exposes GitHubToken(), NotionToken(), GrafanaToken(), GrafanaBaseURL(), PostHogAPIKey(), PostHogHost(), SignozAPIKey(), SignozBaseURL()
cmd/server/main.go: parses --config flag (default ./config.yaml), loads config + .env, prints masked config summary, exits cleanly
cmd/tui/main.go: parses --server flag (default http://localhost:8080), prints server address, exits cleanly

All dependencies added: github.com/go-chi/chi/v5, github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea, github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss, modernc.org/sqlite, github.com/golang-migrate/migrate/v4, github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5, golang.org/x/crypto, gopkg.in/yaml.v3, github.com/google/go-github/v60, github.com/jomei/notionapi
go build ./... succeeds with no errors
Both binaries run and exit cleanly (exit code 0)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Database Migrations (0001–0008)
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need all SQLite migration files with up/down SQL so that the database schema is fully defined and versioned.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration files in internal/store/migrations/ numbered 0001–0008, each with .up.sql and .down.sql, embedded via //go:embed
0001_users.up.sql: users table with id, username (UNIQUE), password_hash, role CHECK(role IN ('view','edit')), created_at
0002_teams.up.sql: teams table and team_members table with foreign key to teams ON DELETE CASCADE

0003_source_catalogue.up.sql: source_catalogue table with UNIQUE(source_type, external_id); valid source_type values: github_repo, notion_page, notion_db, grafana_panel, posthog_panel, signoz_panel; valid status values: untagged, configured, ignored
0004_source_configs.up.sql: source_configs table with UNIQUE(catalogue_id, team_id, purpose); valid purpose values: current_plan, next_plan, goals, metrics_panel, org_goals, org_milestones
0005_annotations.up.sql: annotations table with tier CHECK(tier IN ('item','team')), archived INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
0006_ai_cache.up.sql: ai_cache table with UNIQUE(input_hash, pipeline, team_id); valid pipeline values: sprint_parse, concerns, goal_extraction, workload, velocity, alignment, discovery_suggestion
0007_sync_runs_sprint_meta.up.sql: sync_runs table with scope CHECK and status CHECK; sprint_meta table with UNIQUE(team_id, plan_type) and plan_type CHECK(plan_type IN ('current','next'))
0008_refresh_tokens.up.sql: refresh_tokens table with token_hash UNIQUE, expires_at
All .down.sql files drop the corresponding tables in reverse order
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Store Infrastructure and Models
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the Store struct, migration runner, and Go model structs so that all database access has a typed foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/store/store.go: Store wraps *sql.DB; New(path string) (*Store, error) opens DB, enables WAL mode (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL), enables foreign keys (PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON), runs migrations via golang-migrate wired with modernc.org/sqlite driver
store.Migrate(db *sql.DB) error applies all pending migrations on startup

internal/store/models.go: one Go struct per table with field names matching column names exactly (User, Team, TeamMember, SourceCatalogue, SourceConfig, Annotation, AICache, SyncRun, SprintMeta, RefreshToken)
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auth Utilities: Password Hashing and JWT
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need bcrypt password utilities and JWT issuance/validation so that the auth system has a cryptographic foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/auth/password.go: HashPassword(plain string) (string, error) with bcrypt cost 12; CheckPassword(hash, plain string) bool

internal/auth/jwt.go: Claims struct with Sub, Role, exp; IssueToken(username, role, secret string) (string, error) with 1-hour expiry; IssueRefreshToken() (rawToken string, hash string, err error) with random 32 bytes returning raw token + SHA-256 hex hash; ValidateToken(tokenStr, secret string) (*Claims, error)
Unit tests: JWT issue + validate round-trip passes; expired token rejected; password hash + check works; two IssueRefreshToken calls produce different raw tokens and hashes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Auth Middleware and Bootstrap
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need JWT middleware for route protection and an admin bootstrap function so that the server can enforce authentication and seed the first user.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/auth/bootstrap.go: Bootstrap(store *store.Store, cfg *config.Config) error; if users table is empty, insert admin with username=cfg.Auth.AdminUsername, password_hash=cfg.Auth.AdminPasswordHash (store directly, do not re-hash), role='edit'

internal/api/middleware.go: RequireAuth(secret string) func(http.Handler) http.Handler validates Authorization: Bearer <token>, sets username and role in request context using typed context key, returns 401 on invalid/missing token
RequireRole(role string) func(http.Handler) http.Handler checks context role, returns 403 if insufficient; 'edit' required for mutations, 'view' sufficient for reads
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: API Skeleton and Auth Endpoints
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need an HTTP server with chi router, working auth endpoints, and all other routes returning 501 so that the API surface is established and auth can be tested end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/api/router.go: NewRouter(deps *Deps) http.Handler; Deps struct carries Store *store.Store, Config *config.Config, Engine SyncEngine (interface), Pipeline PipelineRunner (interface); public routes: POST /auth/login, POST /auth/refresh; all other routes under RequireAuth; mutation routes additionally under RequireRole('edit')
internal/api/response.go: writeJSON, writeError (writes {"error": "message"}), readJSON with 1MB size limit

internal/api/auth.go: POST /auth/login reads {username, password}, checks password, issues access + refresh tokens, stores refresh token hash in DB, returns {token, refresh_token}, 401 on bad credentials
POST /auth/refresh reads {refresh_token}, hashes it, validates expiry, issues new access token, rotates refresh token, returns {token, refresh_token}, 401 on invalid/expired

Stub handler files each returning writeError(w, 501, 'not implemented'): api/org.go (GET /org/overview), api/teams.go (GET /teams, GET /teams/{id}/sprint, GET /teams/{id}/goals, GET /teams/{id}/workload, GET /teams/{id}/velocity, GET /teams/{id}/metrics), api/config_teams.go, api/config_sources.go, api/config_annotations.go, api/config_users.go, api/sync.go, api/annotations.go with all routes listed in the PRD
cmd/server/main.go updated: opens store, runs migrations, runs bootstrap, wires Deps, starts HTTP server on configured port
cmd/server/main.go supports hash-password subcommand: checks os.Args[1] == 'hash-password' before normal startup, reads password from stdin, prints bcrypt hash (cost 12) to stdout, exits
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GitHub Source Connector
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the shared Discoverer interface and the GitHub connector with all fetch methods so that GitHub repos can be discovered and queried.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/connector/connector.go: DiscoveredItem struct with SourceType, ExternalID, Title, URL, SourceMeta map[string]any, Excerpt string; Discoverer interface with Discover(ctx, target string) ([]DiscoveredItem, error)

internal/connector/github/: New(token string) *Client using google/go-github with token-authenticated transport; GraphQL client as plain net/http with Authorization: Bearer hitting https://api.github.com/graphql
Discover(ctx, target) enumerates labels, GitHub Projects (GraphQL ProjectV2), .md files (tree API filtered to .md); returns one DiscoveredItem per label, project, and .md file
FetchIssues(ctx, owner, repo, label string, since time.Time) ([]*github.Issue, error) using search API q=repo:{owner}/{repo}+label:{label}+updated:>{since}
FetchDraftIssues(ctx, owner, repo, projectID, teamAreaValue string) ([]DraftIssue, error) via GraphQL ProjectV2Items filtered by Team/Area field and contentType: DraftIssue
FetchMergedPRs(ctx, owner, repo string, since time.Time) ([]*github.PullRequest, error) listing closed PRs, filtering client-side by merged_at > since
FetchCommits(ctx, owner, repo string, since, until time.Time, authors []string) ([]*github.RepositoryCommit, error) with since/until, filtered client-side by author login
FetchMarkdownFile(ctx, owner, repo, path string) (content string, sha string, err error)

AutoTagIssues(ctx, owner, repo, projectID string, teamLabelMap map[string]string) error pages all project items; for each item with Team/Area set but missing label on linked issue, applies label via REST
Connector returns clear error if GITHUB_TOKEN credential is missing (not panic)
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Notion Source Connector
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the Notion connector with discovery and typed fetch methods so that Notion pages and databases can be queried.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/connector/notion/: New(token string) *Client wrapping jomei/notionapi
Discover(ctx, workspaceHint string) ([]DiscoveredItem, error) via POST /search with empty query; returns one item per page (notion_page) and database (notion_db)

FetchPage(ctx, pageID string) (content string, lastEditedTime time.Time, err error) fetches blocks recursively, converts to plain text: headings → # text, bullets → - text, paragraphs → plain text, toggles → > text

FetchDatabase(ctx, dbID string, updatedAfter time.Time) ([]DatabaseRow, error) queries with filter {timestamp: last_edited_time, after: updatedAfter}; each DatabaseRow has title property + linked page content fetched recursively
Connector returns clear error if NOTION_TOKEN credential is missing
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

acceptanceCriteria: internal/connector/grafana/: New(baseURL, token string) *Client; Discover(ctx, dashboardURL string) extracts dashboard UID from URL, calls GET /api/dashboards/uid/:uid, returns one DiscoveredItem per panel; FetchPanel(ctx, dashboardUID, panelID string, from, to time.Time) (PanelData, error) via /api/ds/query

internal/connector/posthog/: New(host, apiKey string) *Client; Discover(ctx, projectID string) via GET /api/projects/:id/dashboards/, returns one item per insight/tile; FetchInsight(ctx, projectID, insightID string) (InsightResult, error) via GET /api/projects/:id/insights/:id/

internal/connector/signoz/: New(baseURL, apiKey string) *Client; Discover(ctx, target string) enumerates dashboards via SigNoz dashboard list API; FetchPanel(ctx, dashboardID, panelID string) (PanelData, error)
All three connectors return clear error if required credentials are missing
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AI Generator Interface and Providers
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the Generator interface and both provider implementations (Anthropic REST and ClaudeCode subprocess) so that AI generation can be called at runtime.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/ai/generator.go: Generator interface with Generate(ctx context.Context, prompt string) (string, error)

internal/ai/anthropic.go: AnthropicProvider with apiKey and model fields; Generate posts to https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages with body {model, max_tokens: 4096, messages: [{role: user, content: prompt}]}, headers x-api-key, anthropic-version: 2023-06-01, content-type: application/json; parses response and returns content[0].text; ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var overrides config api_key if set

internal/ai/claude_code.go: ClaudeCodeProvider with binaryPath and model fields; Generate runs: claude --print --dangerously-skip-permissions --output-format stream-json [--model MODEL] PROMPT; streams stdout line by line; collects text from events where type is 'result' or 'content_block_delta' (field delta.text); returns concatenated text; on non-zero exit returns stderr content as error
internal/ai/factory.go: New(cfg config.AIConfig) (Generator, error) returns ClaudeCodeProvider if cfg.Provider == 'claude-code', AnthropicProvider if 'anthropic', error otherwise
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AI Cached Generator
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the CachedGenerator wrapper so that AI calls are deduplicated via SHA-256 of canonical inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/ai/cached_generator.go: CachedGenerator wraps a Generator and *store.Store

Generate(ctx, pipeline string, teamID *int64, inputs map[string]any, annotations []store.Annotation) (string, error): serializes inputs + annotations to canonical JSON (keys sorted alphabetically at every level), computes SHA-256 hex as inputHash, checks store.GetCacheEntry, returns cached output on hit without calling inner Generator
On cache miss: calls inner Generator.Generate with full prompt, stores result via store.SetCacheEntry, returns result
Unit tests with mock Generator: cache hit returns stored value without calling mock; cache miss calls mock and stores result; different inputs produce different hashes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: AI Pipelines: sprint_parse and goal_extraction
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the sprint_parse and goal_extraction pipelines with full prompts and typed result structs so that sprint metadata and goals can be extracted from documents.
acceptanceCriteria: All pipelines use shared prompt wrapper: 'You are analyzing a software team's status data. Respond ONLY with valid JSON matching this schema: <schema>{SCHEMA}</schema> [optional annotations block] <inputs>{INPUTS_JSON}</inputs>'

internal/pipeline/sprint_parse.go: input {sprint_plan_text}; output schema {start_date, total_sprints, current_sprint, goals}; start_date may be null; after successful run calls store.UpsertSprintMeta with parsed values; SprintParseResult struct defined
internal/pipeline/goal_extraction.go: input {goals_doc_text, sprint_plan_text}; output schema {goals: [{text, source}]}; source is 'goals_doc' or 'sprint_plan'; GoalExtractionResult struct defined
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AI Pipelines: concerns and workload_estimation
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the concerns and workload_estimation pipelines so that risks and resource loads can be identified from sprint data.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/pipeline/concerns.go: input {open_issues, merged_prs, sprint_plan_text, extracted_goals, sprint_meta}; active annotations (non-archived) appended in annotations block; output schema {concerns: [{key, summary, explanation, severity}]}; severity values: high, medium, low; each concern has stable key derived from subject; ConcernsResult struct defined

internal/pipeline/workload.go: input {members: [{name, open_issues, merged_prs, recent_commits}], sprint_window: {start, end}, standard_sprint_days}; output schema {members: [{name, estimated_days, label}]}; label thresholds: LOW < 3, NORMAL 3-5, HIGH > 5; WorkloadResult struct defined
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: AI Pipelines: velocity_analysis, goal_alignment, and discovery_suggestion
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the velocity, alignment, and discovery pipelines so that sprint velocity, org alignment, and source tagging suggestions are available.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/pipeline/velocity.go: input {sprints: [{label, closed_issues, merged_prs, commit_count}]} (up to 4 sprints); output schema {sprints: [{label, score, breakdown: {issues, prs, commits}}]}; prompt instructs Claude to weight by difficulty to normalize score; VelocityResult struct defined
internal/pipeline/alignment.go: input {org_goals_text, team_goals: {team_id: [goals]}}; output schema {alignments: [{team_id, aligned, notes}], flags: [...]}; AlignmentResult struct defined

internal/pipeline/discovery.go: input {title, excerpt} (excerpt = first 500 chars); output schema {suggested_purpose, confidence, reasoning}; valid suggested_purpose: current_plan, next_plan, goals, metrics_panel, org_goals, org_milestones, unknown; valid confidence: high, medium, low; DiscoverySuggestionResult struct defined
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pipeline Runner
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the pipeline Runner struct with one typed method per pipeline so that the sync engine can call pipelines with structured inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/pipeline/runner.go: Runner struct holds *ai.CachedGenerator and *store.Store
RunSprintParse(ctx, teamID int64, sprintPlanText string) (*SprintParseResult, error)
RunGoalExtraction(ctx, teamID int64, goalsDocText, sprintPlanText string) (*GoalExtractionResult, error)
RunConcerns(ctx, teamID int64, input ConcernsInput) (*ConcernsResult, error)
RunWorkloadEstimation(ctx, teamID int64, input WorkloadInput) (*WorkloadResult, error)
RunVelocityAnalysis(ctx, teamID int64, input VelocityInput) (*VelocityResult, error)
RunGoalAlignment(ctx, orgGoalsText string, teamGoals map[int64][]string) (*AlignmentResult, error)
RunDiscoverySuggestion(ctx, title, excerpt string) (*DiscoverySuggestionResult, error)

Each method fetches active (archived=0) annotations for scope, assembles prompt using shared wrapper, calls CachedGenerator.Generate, unmarshals JSON response into typed result struct; returns ErrInvalidAIOutput if response is not valid JSON
Integration test: RunSprintParse with a short sample text using ClaudeCodeProvider returns non-nil result with at least one goal
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sync Discovery Engine
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the discovery pass in the sync engine so that new sources can be catalogued and AI-suggested with a purpose.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/sync/engine.go: Engine struct with store *store.Store, github/notion/grafana/posthog/signoz clients, pipeline *pipeline.Runner; New(...) *Engine constructor
internal/sync/discover.go: (e *Engine) Discover(ctx, scope, target string) (syncRunID int64, err error): inserts sync_runs row with status='running', launches goroutine

Goroutine: calls appropriate connector's Discover based on scope (notion_workspace, github_repo, metrics_url); for each DiscoveredItem calls store.UpsertCatalogueItem (update metadata if exists, insert with status='untagged' if new); for each NEW item calls pipeline.RunDiscoverySuggestion and sets ai_suggested_purpose via store.UpdateCatalogueAISuggestion; calls store.UpdateSyncRun('completed', nil) on success or 'failed' with error string on failure
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sync Engine (Incremental Sync)
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the incremental sync engine so that configured sources are fetched and all relevant AI pipelines are triggered.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/sync/sync.go: (e *Engine) Sync(ctx, scope string, teamID *int64) (syncRunID int64, err error)
Checks for existing 'running' sync run for same scope+teamID; returns existing syncRunID without creating new run if already running
Inserts sync_runs row with status='running', launches goroutine
Goroutine: loads source_configs for scope; for each source fetches incrementally using connector method with source_catalogue.updated_at as since; updates source_catalogue.updated_at on success
Runs pipelines in order: sprint_parse → goal_extraction → concerns → workload_estimation → velocity_analysis (cache handles dedup; always call even if data unchanged)
If scope == 'org': also runs goal_alignment with all teams' extracted goals + org goals
Partial source failures collected per-source in error JSON; do not abort the run; store.UpdateSyncRun('completed', nil) on success or 'failed' with error JSON on failure
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Sync API Handlers
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the sync API endpoints wired to the engine so that the TUI can trigger and poll sync runs.
acceptanceCriteria: api/sync.go stubs replaced: POST /sync validates scope and team_id (required when scope='team'), checks for existing running sync run, returns 409 {"error": "sync already running"} if busy, calls engine.Sync, returns {"sync_run_id": N}
GET /sync/{run_id} returns the sync_runs row as JSON
POST /config/sources/discover validates scope + target, calls engine.Discover, returns {"sync_run_id": N}
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: REST API: Org Overview and Teams List
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need GET /org/overview and GET /teams implemented so that the TUI can display the top-level dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /org/overview: loads all teams; for each team loads latest sprint_meta, cached concerns output, cached workload output; loads goal_alignment output; computes cross-team per-member workload aggregate; returns {teams: [{id, name, current_sprint, total_sprints, risk_level, focus, last_synced_at}], workload: [{name, total_days, label, breakdown: {team_name: days}}], goal_alignment: {alignments, flags}, last_synced_at}
GET /teams: returns [{id, name, members: [{id, display_name, github_username, notion_user_id}]}]
Both endpoints return last_synced_at from the most recent completed sync_runs row for the relevant scope
Before first sync, endpoints return 200 with empty arrays/null fields and last_synced_at: null
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: REST API: Team Data Endpoints
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need all team-specific data endpoints implemented so that the TUI can drill into sprint, goals, workload, velocity, and metrics for a team.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /teams/{id}/sprint: computes current_sprint as floor(days_elapsed/7)+1 where days_elapsed=today-start_date; returns {plan_type, start_date, current_sprint, total_sprints, start_date_missing, next_plan_start_risk, goals, last_synced_at}; start_date_missing: true when sprint_meta.start_date is NULL; next_plan_start_risk: true when total_sprints > 4 AND next-plan source_config exists for team
GET /teams/{id}/goals: returns {goals: [{text, source}], concerns: [{key, summary, explanation, severity, annotation_id}], last_synced_at} from cached pipeline outputs
GET /teams/{id}/workload: returns {members: [{name, estimated_days, label}], last_synced_at} from cache
GET /teams/{id}/velocity: returns {sprints: [{label, score, breakdown: {issues, prs, commits}}], last_synced_at} from cache
GET /teams/{id}/metrics: loads configured metrics_panel source configs for team, returns panel data {panels: [{title, value, panel_id}]}
All endpoints return 200 with empty data and last_synced_at: null before first sync
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

POST /config/teams accepts {name}, creates team, returns team JSON
PUT /config/teams/{id} accepts {name}, updates team, returns updated team

userStories.title: REST API: Config Users CRUD and Inline Annotations
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the config users endpoints and the inline annotations mount point so that user management and in-context annotation work via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /config/users returns list of users (edit role only)
POST /config/users accepts {username, password, role}, hashes password before storing (edit role only)

userStories.title: Plan Rollover Logic
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need plan rollover logic so that when a new current plan is tagged, all item-tier annotations for the old plan are archived and sprint metadata is reset.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /config/sources/{id} with purpose='current_plan': looks up existing source_configs row for (team_id, purpose='current_plan'); if it differs from the new one: calls store.ArchiveItemAnnotationsForPlan(teamID) (UPDATE annotations SET archived=1 WHERE team_id=? AND tier='item' AND archived=0), deletes old source_configs row, inserts new source_configs row, triggers sprint_parse pipeline for new document (or marks for re-parse on next sync)
If no previous current plan exists: simply inserts new source_configs row
team-tier annotations for the same team are untouched (SQL WHERE tier='item' ensures this)
Archived annotations visible in GET /config/annotations with archived: true but excluded from all pipeline inputs (pipeline.Runner methods filter WHERE archived=0)
Unit test: create team, create item and team annotations, trigger rollover, verify item annotations have archived=1 and team annotations have archived=0
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: TUI Client and App Architecture
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the TUI HTTP client and Bubble Tea app scaffolding so that the TUI has a foundation for all views.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/tui/client/client.go: typed HTTP client; reads JWT from ~/.dashboard/token (JSON with token and refresh_token fields); on 401 attempts token refresh; if refresh fails clears ~/.dashboard/token and returns error to trigger login screen

Client methods: Login, GetOrgOverview, GetTeams, GetSprint, GetGoals, GetWorkload, GetVelocity, GetMetrics, PostSync, GetSyncRun, PostAnnotation, PutAnnotation, DeleteAnnotation, GetConfigSources, PutConfigSource, PostDiscover, GetConfigAnnotations, GetConfigUsers, PostConfigUser, PutConfigUser, DeleteConfigUser
internal/tui/app.go: App struct with views []tea.Model (stack), client *client.Client, syncPoller *SyncPoller; Enter pushes view; Esc/Backspace pops view; q quits only when stack depth is 1
cmd/tui/main.go updated to launch Bubble Tea program with App as root model
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TUI Login View
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the TUI login screen so that users can authenticate from the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/tui/views/login.go: username + password text inputs using Bubble Tea text input component
Submit with Enter; calls client.Login; on success stores {token, refresh_token} to ~/.dashboard/token as JSON
On failure shows error message below the form
On startup, app checks if ~/.dashboard/token exists and contains a valid (non-expired) token; if not, pushes login view
App also presents login view when server returns 401 and refresh fails
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

j/k navigate up/down through team cards
Enter on a team pushes Team View
Bottom row shows org-level metrics summary
Footer shows Last synced: <timestamp> (or Never synced)
R triggers full org sync and shows sync banner
Before first sync shows: No data yet. Press r to sync this team (or R for full org).

Lists plan goals below

userStories.title: TUI Sync Banner Component
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need the sync banner component and SyncPoller so that in-progress syncs are visible in all views.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/tui/components/banner.go: renders at top of every view when sync run is active: [ Sync in progress... (Team Name) ]
SyncPoller goroutine polls GET /sync/{run_id} every 2 seconds via time.Ticker; sends SyncDoneMsg or SyncFailedMsg to Bubble Tea program via tea.Program.Send
On completed: banner disappears
On failed: banner turns red showing [ Sync failed: <error> ]  Press Enter to dismiss
App model handles SyncDoneMsg and SyncFailedMsg messages to update banner state
go build ./... succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Server Wiring and Integration
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need graceful shutdown, cache pruning, auto-tagger scheduling, and the admin autotag endpoint so that the server runs robustly in production.
acceptanceCriteria: cmd/server/main.go: signal.NotifyContext(ctx, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM) for graceful shutdown; http.Server.Shutdown(ctx) called on signal
In-progress sync goroutines tracked with sync.WaitGroup; shutdown waits up to 30 seconds before forcing exit
On server startup: store.PruneStaleCache(30 * 24 * time.Hour) called to delete ai_cache entries older than 30 days
time.Ticker every 12 hours calls engine.AutoTag(ctx) for all configured GitHub repos; engine.AutoTag calls github.AutoTagIssues for each configured repo+project
GET /admin/autotag endpoint (edit role only) triggers engine.AutoTag immediately, returns {"status": "ok"}
go build ./... succeeds with no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integration Smoke Test Script
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need a smoke test shell script so that the full system can be verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/smoke_test.sh: starts server in background
Logs in with admin credentials, saves token to variable
Calls GET /teams — expects HTTP 200
Calls GET /org/overview — expects HTTP 200
Calls POST /sync with scope=org — expects 200 with sync_run_id
Polls GET /sync/{run_id} every 2 seconds until status is completed or 60-second timeout
Calls GET /teams/{id}/sprint — expects HTTP 200
Prints PASS or FAIL; on failure prints curl output for the failing request
Script is executable (chmod +x)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Hardening: Missing Data and Concurrent Sync Edge Cases
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need edge case handling for missing sprint dates, extended sprints, and concurrent sync prevention so that the system is robust.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /teams/{id}/sprint returns start_date_missing: true when sprint_meta.start_date is NULL; no panic or 500 error
TUI Sprint view shows missing-date warning in amber when start_date_missing is true
When total_sprints > 4 and next-plan source config exists for team, GET /teams/{id}/sprint returns next_plan_start_risk: true; TUI shows Week N of M with M highlighted in amber if M > 4
POST /sync returns 409 {"error": "sync already running for this scope"} if running sync exists for same scope+teamID; no second goroutine started
Per-source fetch errors collected during sync: sync run still completes for other sources; sync_runs.error stores JSON {"source_id": "error message"}; TUI sync error banner lists which sources failed
go test ./... passes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Hardening: Error Handling, Credential Checks, and Race Condition Tests
userStories.description: As an AI coding agent, I need robust error handling for AI failures, missing credentials, first-run state, and JWT expiry, plus race condition tests so that the system is production-ready.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline runner returns typed ErrInvalidAIOutput when Claude's response is not valid JSON; logs raw output at ERROR level; TUI shows 'AI processing error — try syncing again.' with dismiss option (d or Enter); no crash
Each connector checks required env vars in constructor; if missing returns ErrCredentialsMissing with var name; discovery/sync surfaces clear error in TUI (not panic or generic 500)

Before any sync completed, all data endpoints (/teams/{id}/sprint, /goals, /workload, /velocity, /metrics) return 200 with empty arrays/null fields and last_synced_at: null; TUI shows no-data placeholder in each view
TUI client automatically attempts refresh on 401; if refresh fails clears ~/.dashboard/token and presents login screen; no crash
go test -race ./... passes with no data races detected
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
